message.content:
You are authoring an AsciiDoc procedural sub-spec for the Recipe Bottle project. The target file ALREADY EXISTS on disk at the path below — read it for the orphan-knight content that needs wholesale replacement (its old content is the "knight" semantics; you are replacing it with "invest" semantics). The replacement must preserve the AXHO* block grammar and procedural detail discipline. Pure .adoc, no code.

TARGET FILE (overwrite with Write tool — file currently holds old "director_knight" content as a starting reference):
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDK-director_invest.adoc

NEW BEHAVIORAL CONTRACT (from RBS0:1058-1069, BBAAQ Surface 2):
- Operation: invest a director service account (was "knight")
- Anchor: [[rbtgo_director_invest]]
- Singleton-per-role: ZERO instance argument from operator
- SA name derived from role and depot moniker (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied):
    accountId = "${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}"
    email     = "${accountId}@${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com"
- Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively (the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires)
- Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key.

DELTAS FROM ORPHAN (compare current file content):
- DELETE the //axhopt_typed_parameter block (zero-arg now)
- DELETE the //axhoq_precondition for `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` env var
- DELETE the final "Display Installation Instructions" block (with the cp command and warn about `{rbrr_director_rbra_file}` already existing) — replaced by assay-slot output discipline
- ADD a step right after authenticate: probe whether the SA already exists (HTTP GET on the email-derived SA path) and {rbbc_fatal} if it does, with message instructing the operator to divest first
- REPLACE the SA-name derivation step: instead of taking «INPUT_INSTANCE» argument, derive «DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME» as `${RBCC_role_director}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (regime-kindled, link via {rbrr_depot_moniker})
- RETIRE references to `{rbrr_depot_project_id}` — that field was removed in BBAAY. Use the linked term {rbtgi_depot_project} where the orphan currently writes `{rbrr_depot_project_id}`. The depot project ID is now derived as RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID from (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER).
- REPLACE the displayName/description text: drop "(«INPUT_INSTANCE»)" suffixes — the SA is singleton per-role per-depot. Suggest:
    displayName: `Recipe Bottle Director`
    description: `Create/destroy container images for depot ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`
- KEEP the SA propagation polling step
- KEEP the pristine key-state preflight step
- KEEP the keys.create step with retry-on-404 and the {rbtoe_rbra_generate} call
- KEEP all IAM grant steps (project-level cloudbuild.builds.editor + viewer + workerPoolUser, mason serviceAccountUser, build-bucket access, GAR repoAdmin) — these are the director's permission set
- REPLACE the typed-output `{rbtgi_rbra_file}` final step with one that writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot via the producer-output discipline (one short sentence — this is contract-level, not implementation-detailed). Reference the linked term {burd_assay} if appropriate, otherwise use plain text "assay output slot".
- UPDATE the //axhoc_completion prose to describe successful invest semantics ("invests director service account" not "knights")

PRESERVE FROM ORPHAN:
- All API URL link:https://...[...] references to GCP REST docs (iam.serviceAccounts.create, iam.serviceAccounts.get, serviceAccounts.keys.list/create, projects.get, repositories.getIamPolicy/setIamPolicy)
- HTTP response code expectations (200, 404 handling)
- All NOTE blocks about email-vs-UID propagation, 404-during-create propagation, complete-policy-write pattern, cross-references to <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>
- Linked term references: {rbtr_governor}, {rbtr_director}, {rbtr_mason}, {rbtgi_repository}, {rbtgi_build_bucket}, {giam_service_account}, {rbtoe_governor_authenticate}, {rbtoe_rbra_generate}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_project}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}
- The {rbbc_*} action-verb idiom in step bullets
- Policy version: 3 marker on IAM steps

GOLD-STANDARD SHAPE TO MATCH:
Read these files for shape/depth reference:
- /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc (gold standard)
- /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc (similar shape)

ANCHOR PLACEMENT:
The first lines of the new file MUST be:
```
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_director_invest}
```
(The `//axhob_operation` opener carries the anchor implicitly via the operation quoin reference. Do NOT add a separate `[[rbtgo_director_invest]]` line — see how RBSDE/RBSDV open their files; the anchor is established by the //axhob_operation + linked-term opener pattern.)

VERIFY YOUR OUTPUT BY COMPARING SHAPE WITH:
- Opening: //axhob_operation + linked-term operation name (matches RBSDE:1-2)
- All step blocks use //axhos_step opener
- All linked-terms use {curly_braces} not raw uppercase
- API endpoints have full link:URL[anchor-text] form
- File closes with //axhoc_completion + summary prose

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT touch any other files. Do NOT make any commits. Just write to the target file.
- Do NOT include code blocks or shell snippets — this is pure spec prose.
- Length will be similar to the orphan (~165 lines). Aim for procedural completeness without padding.

After writing, report in 4 lines max:
1. Final line count
2. The first 3 lines of the file (to verify anchor opener)
3. Number of //axhos_step blocks
4. Confirmation that {rbrr_depot_project_id} does not appear in your output

content.text: I'll read the target file and gold standard references first.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_knight}
3	
4	The
5	{rbtr_governor}
6	knights a
7	{rbtr_director}
8	{giam_service_account}
9	with build management permissions:
10	
11	//axhopt_typed_parameter
12	{rbtr_director}
13	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
14	.. {rbbc_require}
15	`«INPUT_INSTANCE»` provided as first argument
16	.. {rbbc_store}
17	`«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»` as `director-«INPUT_INSTANCE»`
18	.. {rbbc_store}
19	`«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` as `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»@{rbrr_depot_project_id}.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
20	
21	//axhoq_precondition
22	* {rbbc_require}
23	`BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` environment variable set and directory exists
24	
25	//axhos_step
26	. *Authenticate as
27	{rbtr_governor}*:
28	.. Execute
29	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
30	.. {rbbc_store}
31	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
32	
33	//axhos_step
34	. *Create
35	{rbtr_director}
36	Service Account*:
37	.. {rbbc_call}
38	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
39	... accountId: `«DIRECTOR_ACCOUNT_NAME»`
40	... displayName: `Recipe Bottle Director («INPUT_INSTANCE»)`
41	... description: `Create/destroy container images for «INPUT_INSTANCE»`
42	... project:
43	{rbrr_depot_project_id}
44	.. {rbbc_require}
45	HTTP 200 response
46	.. {rbbc_store}
47	`«DIRECTOR_UNIQUE_ID»` from response `.uniqueId` field
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Verify Service Account Propagation*:
51	.. {rbbc_call}
52	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
53	.. {rbbc_poll}
54	with backoff until HTTP 200 (newly-created SA may not be immediately resolvable by email)
55	.. NOTE: Poll must use the email address, not the numeric ID. The email-based lookup path has independent propagation from the UID-based path, and subsequent steps (keys.list, keys.create) all use the email address. Polling by UID can succeed while the email path still returns 404.
56	.. {rbbc_require}
57	service account accessible by email
58	
59	//axhos_step
60	. *Preflight: Ensure Pristine Key State*:
61	.. {rbbc_call}
62	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/list[serviceAccounts.keys.list] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»`
63	.. {rbbc_store}
64	`«EXISTING_KEYS»` from response `.keys[]`
65	.. {rbbc_require}
66	no keys with `keyType: USER_MANAGED` exist
67	.. {rbbc_fatal}
68	if USER_MANAGED keys found: "Manual cleanup required via Console"
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Generate Service Account Key*:
72	.. {rbbc_call}
73	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/create[serviceAccounts.keys.create] for `«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with:
74	... privateKeyType: `TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE`
75	.. Retry up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX` times on HTTP 404 with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts
76	.. NOTE: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) may succeed while keys.create POST returns 404 "does not exist" due to write-path propagation delay. Google link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[documents]: "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account." The 404 confirms no key was created, making retry safe. See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
77	.. {rbbc_store}
78	`«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` from response `.privateKeyData` field
79	.. {rbtoe_rbra_generate}
80	converts `«DIRECTOR_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` to `«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»`
81	
82	//axhos_step
83	. *Get Depot Project Number*:
84	.. {rbbc_call}
85	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for
86	{rbrr_depot_project_id}
87	.. {rbbc_store}
88	`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.projectNumber` field
89	
90	//axhos_step
91	. *Grant Project-Level Permissions*:
92	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} for
93	{rbrr_depot_project_id}
94	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
95	... `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor` (submit and manage builds)
96	... `roles/viewer` (read project resources)
97	... `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` (use private pool for vouch `builds.create`)
98	.. Policy version: 3
99	.. {rbbc_require}
100	IAM policy update successful
101	
102	//axhos_step
103	. *Grant
104	{rbtr_mason}
105	Impersonation*:
106	.. NOTE:
107	{rbtr_director}
108	submits builds that execute as
109	{rbtr_mason}.
110	This requires `serviceAccountUser` permission on
111	{rbtr_mason}.
112	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on
113	{rbtr_mason}
114	service account
115	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`
116	.. {rbbc_require}
117	IAM binding successful
118	
119	//axhos_step
120	. *Grant
121	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
122	Access*:
123	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on
124	{rbtgi_build_bucket}
125	.. Add `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with roles:
126	... `roles/storage.objectCreator` (upload sources)
127	... `roles/storage.objectViewer` (verify uploads)
128	.. {rbbc_require}
129	bucket IAM update successful
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Grant
133	{rbtgi_repository}
134	Admin Access*:
135	.. {rbbc_call}
136	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/getIamPolicy[repositories.getIamPolicy] via HTTP GET with `options.requestedPolicyVersion=3` query parameter
137	.. NOTE: {gar_registry} proto specifies GET for `getIamPolicy` with no `additional_bindings` for POST. Using POST fails intermittently with HTTP 404 (transcoding layer rejection, not missing repository). See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
138	.. {rbbc_require}
139	HTTP 200 — 404 means repository does not exist (real error, not empty policy)
140	.. {rbbc_call}
141	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/setIamPolicy[repositories.setIamPolicy] for
142	{rbtgi_repository}
143	.. *Complete policy write*: Add ALL expected bindings to the read policy, regardless of what the read returned:
144	... `serviceAccount:«DIRECTOR_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin` — Director manages images
145	... `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer` — Mason pushes images at build time
146	.. NOTE: Same complete-policy-write pattern as the secret IAM step. Prevents stale read from overwriting Mason's writer binding set by {rbtgo_depot_levy}.
147	.. {rbbc_require}
148	repository IAM set with all expected bindings
149	
150	//axhoot_typed_output
151	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
152	. *Display Installation Instructions*:
153	.. {rbbc_show}
154	Director created successfully
155	.. {rbbc_show}
156	RBRA file location: `«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»`
157	.. {rbbc_show}
158	installation command:
159	... `cp "«DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE»" "{rbrr_director_rbra_file}"`
160	.. {rbbc_warn}
161	if
162	{rbrr_director_rbra_file}
163	already exists
164	
165	//axhoc_completion
166	Successful execution knights director service account.
167

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_depot_levy}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation creates an
5	{rbtgi_depot}
6	- a cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure including the depot project, build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and private worker pool
7	that share their productive lifecycle.
8	
9	The
10	{rbtr_payor}
11	creates a new depot infrastructure from the
12	{rbtgi_payor_project}.
13	The operation accepts no CLI parameters; depot identity is read from the kindled regime as `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
14	
15	//axhos_step
16	. *Authenticate as
17	{rbtr_payor}*:
18	.. Execute
19	{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}
20	
21	//axhos_step
22	. *Validate region against Artifact Registry locations*:
23	.. {rbbc_call}
24	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations/list[locations.list] for the authenticated project
25	.. {rbbc_require}
26	`{rbrr_gcp_region}` present in the returned location set
27	
28	//axhos_step
29	. *Derive Depot Identity*:
30	.. {rbbc_store}
31	`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (resolved by `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`)
32	.. {rbbc_store}
33	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}-gar` (resolved by `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`)
34	.. {rbbc_store}
35	`«WORKER_POOL_STEM»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}-pool` (resolved by `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`)
36	.. {rbbc_store}
37	`«BUILD_BUCKET»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (resolved by `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET`)
38	.. NOTE: Joint length `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}` + `d-` + `${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` ≤ 30 chars (GCP project ID limit) is enforced upstream by `zrbrr_enforce` at regime kindle time, so this step performs no length validation.
39	
40	//axhos_step
41	. *Create Depot Project*:
42	.. {rbbc_submit}
43	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/create[projects.create] with:
44	... projectId: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
45	... displayName: `RB Depot: «RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
46	... parent: `{rbrp_parent_type}s/{rbrp_parent_id}` (if parent_type != `none`)
47	.. {rbbc_await}
48	returned operation
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	project creation successful
51	.. NOTE: Project name collision against the GCP soft-delete graveyard surfaces here as fatal — re-using a moniker after `{rbtgo_depot_unmake}` requires waiting out the 30-day retention window. The operator-visible "fails if exists" property is intentional under the (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) shape.
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Link Billing Account*:
55	.. {rbbc_call}
56	link:https://cloud.google.com/billing/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects/updateBillingInfo[cloudbilling.projects.updateBillingInfo] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
57	.. Set billingAccountName: `billingAccounts/{rbrp_billing_account_id}`
58	.. {rbbc_require}
59	HTTP 200 response confirming billing linkage
60	
61	//axhos_step
62	. *Get Depot Project Number*:
63	.. {rbbc_call}
64	link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
65	.. {rbbc_store}
66	`«PROJECT_NUMBER»` from `.name` field (CRM v3 returns `projects/{number}`)
67	.. {rbbc_require}
68	valid numeric project number after extracting from name
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Enable Depot Project APIs*:
72	.. For each service in [`artifactregistry`, `cloudbuild`, `containeranalysis`, `iam`, `serviceusage`, `storage`]:
73	... Execute
74	{rbtoe_api_enable}
75	with service name and `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
76	.. {rbbc_require}
77	all APIs enabled successfully
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Create Private Worker Pool*:
81	.. {rbbc_call}
82	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.workerPools/create[workerPools.create] with:
83	... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}`
84	... workerPoolId: `«WORKER_POOL_STEM»`
85	... body:
86	+
87	----
88	{
89	  "privatePoolV1Config": {
90	    "workerConfig": {
91	      "machineType": "«RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE»"
92	    }
93	  }
94	}
95	----
96	.. NOTE:
97	{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}
98	is a Compute Engine machine type (e.g., `e2-standard-2`), consumed at pool creation time.
99	Pool scales to zero workers when idle — no idle cost accrues.
100	.. Treat HTTP 409 CONFLICT as success (pool already exists — idempotent)
101	
102	//axhos_step
103	. *Verify IAM Propagation*:
104	.. NOTE: After API enablement, IAM permissions may not be immediately available due to eventual consistency.
105	.. {rbbc_poll}
106	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/list[artifactregistry.repositories.list] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` in `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
107	.. Poll at `RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC` intervals until HTTP 200 or `RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC` timeout
108	.. {rbbc_require}
109	HTTP 200 response confirming IAM propagation complete
110	.. {rbbc_fatal}
111	if timeout: "IAM propagation timeout - artifactregistry API not accessible"
112	
113	//axhos_step
114	. *Create Build Bucket*:
115	.. {rbbc_call}
116	link:https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/insert[storage.buckets.insert] with:
117	... name: `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
118	... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
119	... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
120	... lifecycle rule: delete objects after 1 day
121	
122	//axhos_step
123	. *Create Container Repository*:
124	.. {rbbc_submit}
125	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/create[artifactregistry.repositories.create] with:
126	... repositoryId: `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
127	... format: `DOCKER`
128	... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
129	... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}`
130	.. {rbbc_await}
131	returned operation
132	
133	//axhos_step
134	. *Create
135	{rbtr_mason}
136	Service Account*:
137	.. NOTE: The
138	{rbtr_mason}
139	replaces Cloud Build's implicit service account to provide deterministic permissions and predictable authorization within the depot infrastructure.
140	.. {rbbc_call}
141	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
142	... accountId: `mason-«RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
143	... displayName: `Mason for RB Depot: «RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER»`
144	... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
145	.. {rbbc_store}
146	`«MASON_EMAIL»` from response `.email` field
147	
148	//axhos_step
149	. *Configure
150	{rbtr_mason}
151	Permissions*:
152	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo} on `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
153	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer`
154	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
155	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/storage.objectViewer`
156	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} on `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
157	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/viewer`
158	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/logging.logWriter`
159	
160	//axhos_step
161	. *Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate
162	{rbtr_mason}*:
163	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on `«MASON_EMAIL»`
164	.. Add member: `serviceAccount:service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
165	.. Grant role: `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`
166	.. NOTE: Cloud Build service agent is Google-provisioned (auto-created when Cloud Build API is enabled), not a user-created service account. The `tokenCreator` role enables build steps running as {rbtr_mason} (via the build JSON's `serviceAccount` field) to generate fresh access tokens for GCP API calls — required for GAR authentication (docker login, crane push). This is distinct from `serviceAccountUser` (actAs), which the {rbtr_director} holds on {rbtr_mason} for submitting builds.
167	.. {rbbc_require}
168	IAM binding successful
169	
170	//axhoot_typed_output
171	{rbtgi_depot}
172	. *Confirm Depot Creation*:
173	.. {rbbc_show}
174	single-line success banner naming `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` and the
175	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
176	follow-up hint
177	
178	//axhoot_typed_output
179	{rbtgi_depot}
180	. *Update Depot Tracking*:
181	.. Execute
182	{rbtoe_depot_list_update}
183	to refresh per-moniker depot fact files
184	
185	//axhoc_completion
186	Successful execution creates depot infrastructure with configured build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and private worker pool — all derived from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
187	Proceed to
188	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
189	to create the
190	{rbtr_governor}
191	for this depot.
192

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_vouch}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation vouches for every *pending*
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository},
8	where *pending* means a hallmark whose subtree contains both
9	{rbtga_ark_image}
10	and
11	{rbtga_ark_about}
12	basenames but no
13	{rbtga_ark_vouch}.
14	The
15	{rbtr_director}
16	enumerates the full hallmark corpus via a single GAR REST `packages.list` call (the same shared helper used by
17	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}),
18	identifies pending hallmarks, resolves the vessel name from each hallmark's
19	{rbtga_ark_about}
20	metadata (since vessel identity no longer lives in the GAR path), and dispatches
21	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
22	per hallmark.
23	Mode-awareness (conjure, bind, graft) lives inside
24	{rbtgo_ark_vouch};
25	batch vouch does not branch on mode.
26	
27	NOTE: *Fail-fast semantics.* If
28	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
29	fails for any hallmark, the batch terminates via fatal error at that hallmark.
30	There is no skip-with-warning path and no summary-count-masking of failures: a broken hallmark demands immediate attention, not quiet continuation.
31	
32	The
33	{rbtr_director}
34	batch-vouches arks:
35	
36	//axhos_step
37	. *Authenticate as
38	{rbtr_director}*:
39	.. Execute
40	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
41	.. {rbbc_store}
42	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
43	
44	//axhos_step
45	. *Enumerate Hallmark Packages*:
46	.. {rbbc_call}
47	the shared hallmark enumeration helper — a single
48	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list]
49	call under `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/` (`RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT`), filtered to `<hallmark>/<basename>` shape
50	.. {rbbc_store}
51	`«PAIRS»` as sorted `<hallmark> <basename>` pairs (same shape produced for
52	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally})
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Identify Pending Hallmarks (Pass 1)*:
56	.. Iterate `«PAIRS»` in sort order; each contiguous run of the same hallmark is one
57	{rbtga_hallmark}'s
58	ark inventory.
59	.. For each hallmark, record whether
60	{rbtga_ark_image},
61	{rbtga_ark_about},
62	and
63	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
64	each appear in its run.
65	.. A hallmark is *pending* when `image` and `about` are present and `vouch` is absent.
66	Collect pending hallmarks into `«PENDING»` (in-memory sequence).
67	.. If `«PENDING»` is empty, the procedure completes successfully with no vouches submitted.
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Resolve Vessel and Vouch Per Pending Hallmark (Pass 2)*:
71	.. For each `«HALLMARK»` in `«PENDING»`:
72	... Extract the hallmark's
73	{rbtga_ark_about}
74	artifact (package `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«HALLMARK»/about`, tag `«HALLMARK»`) to a scratch directory via the shared GAR artifact extraction helper
75	... Read `.vessel_name` from the extracted `build_info.json` via `jq`
76	... {rbbc_store}
77	`«VESSEL_DIR»` as `«RBRR_VESSEL_DIR»/«VESSEL_NAME»`
78	... {rbbc_require}
79	`«VESSEL_DIR»` exists on disk — the vessel named in `about` metadata must correspond to a configured vessel in the current regime
80	... {rbbc_fatal}
81	if the `about` artifact cannot be extracted, `build_info.json` is missing, `.vessel_name` is empty, or `«VESSEL_DIR»` does not exist — the `about` artifact is the sole source of vessel identity for this batch; a gap there is a fail-fast stop
82	... Invoke
83	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
84	with `«VESSEL_DIR»` and `«HALLMARK»`.
85	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
86	loads the vessel's
87	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
88	internally and dispatches the appropriate verification (conjure: SLSA DSSE verification; bind: digest-pin; graft: GRAFTED stamp).
89	... {rbbc_fatal}
90	if
91	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
92	fails — the batch terminates at the broken hallmark.
93	
94	//axhoot_typed_output
95	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
96	. *Display Summary*:
97	.. {rbbc_show}
98	`Batch vouch complete — N/N hallmark(s) vouched` where N is the initial pending count
99	
100	//axhoc_completion
101	Successful execution creates one
102	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
103	artifact per pending hallmark (N total, where N = initial pending count).
104	The batch runs sequentially; a failure at any hallmark halts the remainder.
105	+
106	*Scope of vessel resolution*: Reading `vessel_name` from `build_info.json` is a narrow anticipation of the broader vessel-from-metadata pattern (AAL territory) — applied locally inside batch vouch only, because
107	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
108	's signature still takes a vessel directory.
109	Other operations (tally, plumb, abjure) continue to not surface vessel.
110	+
111	*Two-pass shape*: Pass 1 collects pending hallmarks into memory before Pass 2 begins processing.
112	Each
113	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
114	invocation sources credentials and issues Cloud Build calls that would silently consume loop stdin if the pending set were streamed; the in-memory collection decouples enumeration from dispatch.
115	+
116	*Idempotency*: Re-running batch vouch after a successful batch finds an empty pending set (every prior pending hallmark now has `vouch` present) and exits with no action.
117	+
118	*No mode pre-check*: Earlier shapes of this operation pre-probed SLSA provenance for conjure-mode hallmarks to skip-with-warning those lacking provenance.
119	That pattern is gone — all mode-specific verification happens inside
120	{rbtgo_ark_vouch},
121	and any failure there surfaces as a fail-fast stop rather than being absorbed into a summary count.
122
